channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Argentina is back in the World Cup final after a thrilling semifinal win over England
item.description: Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.

(Image credit: Shaun Botterill)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Takeaways from Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Todd Blanche remained composed throughout the hours-long hearing, but faced several testy moments during questioning as he seeks to win the support of all Republican senators on the committee.
item.content:encoded:
Todd Blanche remained composed throughout the hours-long hearing, but faced several testy moments during questioning as he seeks to win the support of all Republican senators on the committee.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Human Rights Watch report details abuse at immigration detention center
item.description: People held in the immigration detention camp at the Fort Bliss military base in Texas have experienced beatings and life-threatening medical neglect, according to a report by Human Rights Watch.
item.content:encoded: People held in the immigration detention camp at the Fort Bliss military base in Texas have experienced beatings and life-threatening medical neglect, according to a report by Human Rights Watch.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Trump relished in being compared to dictators like Hitler and Stalin, journalist says
item.description: The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Todd Blanche's high-stakes confirmation hearing for attorney general is underway
item.description: President Trump's former personal attorney is under the microscope on Capitol Hill. Lawmakers on the Senate Judiciary Committee are questioning Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, whom Trump has nominated for the job permanently.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's former personal attorney is under the microscope on Capitol Hill. Lawmakers on the Senate Judiciary Committee are questioning Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, whom Trump has nominated for the job permanently.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Todd Blanche faces high-stakes confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he's facing a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.
item.content:encoded:
Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he's facing a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Democrats' witness opposing Blanche's confirmation is more than an Epstein survivor
item.description: Dani Bensky and a group of women who were abused by Jeffrey Epstein have formed a bond they call a "survivor sisterhood." They live in two worlds, of advocacy and everyday life and motherhood.
item.content:encoded:
Dani Bensky and a group of women who were abused by Jeffrey Epstein have formed a bond they call a "survivor sisterhood." They live in two worlds, of advocacy and everyday life and motherhood.

(Image credit: Corrie Aune for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Baltimore to overhaul how it responds to calls for mental health and other services
item.description: Baltimore is setting up a fourth branch of first responders in addition to fire, police and EMS. They will handle calls for people needing mental health support, but also connection to city services.
item.content:encoded: Baltimore is setting up a fourth branch of first responders in addition to fire, police and EMS. They will handle calls for people needing mental health support, but also connection to city services.
item.dc:creator: Scott Maucione

item.title: Trump administration's proposal to shift management of grizzly bears causes confusion
item.description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum says he's turning management of threatened grizzly bears in states that share Yellowstone National Park to state governments. What exactly that means is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum says he's turning management of threatened grizzly bears in states that share Yellowstone National Park to state governments. What exactly that means is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Merzbach

item.title: How the federal government is responding to the worsening western fire season
item.description: The bad summer fire season predicted for Western states is in full swing, with more and larger fires predicted. Federal officials say they have adequate resources to fight them, for now.
item.content:encoded: The bad summer fire season predicted for Western states is in full swing, with more and larger fires predicted. Federal officials say they have adequate resources to fight them, for now.
item.dc:creator: Chris Clements
